commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a0e1e7722433f6857bb2ff8b9207e4fbf56df7eb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates models.json planning to omit plaintext provider apiKey values while preserving known non-secret markers, with coverage for explicit, implicit, and merge-mode existing providers.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main writes merged provider apiKey values into models.json, including existing merge-mode plaintext values. I did not run a local generation path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer decision on the upgrade/security tradeoff plus contributor real behavior proof; this is not a safe automated repair lane yet.

target.title: fix(models): omit plaintext api keys from models.json
target.body:
Fixes #11829.

This implements the Layer 1 hardening for `models.json` generation:

- omit plaintext provider `apiKey` values before serializing agent-visible `models.json`
- preserve non-secret auth markers such as known environment variable markers so existing marker-backed providers keep working
- cover explicit providers, implicit providers, and stale plaintext values preserved from an existing merge-mode `models.json`

Validation:

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --reporter=dot src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

TaskBounty: https://www.task-bounty.com/task/fix-security-roadmap-protecting-api-keys-from-agen-uej5sq
target.author: junn-dev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
